message:
feat(service): add service lifecycle management API and extend status code descriptive strings

Introduces the asx/service module with register/start/stop/health/iterate
lifecycle primitives for long-running managed services. Services transition
through REGISTERED → STARTING → RUNNING → STOPPING → STOPPED states with
compile-time capability guards (ASX_HAS_THREADS, ASX_HAS_SIGNALS) that gate
signal-based stop and threaded health checks on browser builds.

Extends asx_status with descriptive string helpers: asx_status_name() returns
short mnemonic (e.g. "OK", "PERM"), asx_status_describe() returns full
human-readable description. Adds status codes ASX_E_SERVICE_STATE and
ASX_E_SERVICE_NOT_FOUND for service module error reporting.

Also adjusts app.h init/shutdown signatures for consistency with the new
service module and adds migration module build scaffolding.

- include/asx/service/service.h: full service lifecycle API surface
- src/service/service.c: implementation with array-based registry
- tests/unit/service/test_service.c: lifecycle, error handling, iterate tests
- include/asx/asx_status.h: add status name/describe, new service error codes
- src/core/status.c: implement status string tables
- include/asx/app/app.h: adjust init/shutdown param counts
- src/migration/migration.c: build scaffolding
- docs/PROFILE_RESOURCE_CLASS_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.md: update service row
- .beads/issues.jsonl: track service tasks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-66l","title":"EPIC: Hard Quality Gates and CI Enforcement","description":"Background:
The plan defines non-negotiable gates. They must be automated and enforced to prevent regressions from entering mainline.

Goal:
Encode all required gates in CI with reproducible artifacts and clear fail reasons.

Gates include:
- portability, OOM semantics, fuzz parity
- embedded matrix, profile parity, semantic-delta budget
- HFT tail/jitter, automotive deadline/watchdog
- crash/restart replay continuity
- warning/static-analysis/bounded-proof gates

Why this serves users:
- transforms policy into enforceable guarantees
- makes trust scalable beyond individual reviewers.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","notes":"Executed API ergonomics validation lane work (bd-56t.5 content) under this quality-gate epic: added make test-vignettes target, CI step+manifest wiring, tests/TEST.md vignette coverage docs, docs/API_ERGONOMICS_VALIDATION.md sign-off, and fixed vignette compile/runtime issues. Validation via rch: make test-vignettes/build/format-check/lint/test pass. Conformance now runs under rch with SEMANTIC_DELTA_ARTIFACT_DIR override support in tools/ci/run_conformance.sh; current remote conformance still fails on existing non-budgetable semantic-delta condition (fail=1, diff_records=1), not a permission failure.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"IvoryGlacier","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.390018086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:30:49.559586930Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:30:49.559560691Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","phase-10","quality-gates"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-1md","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-296","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-66l.1","title":"Implement CI workflow matrix for build, tests, conformance, profiles, fuzz, embedded, and perf","description":"Background:
Quality policy is only real when encoded in CI. Workflow structure must mirror plan-gate structure.

Subtasks:
- Define jobs for check, unit-invariant, conformance, profile-parity, fuzz-parity, embedded-matrix, perf-tail-deadline, and e2e scenario packs.
- Ensure deterministic artifact naming and retention.
- Ensure failure output is actionable and machine-readable where possible.
- Enforce publication of test run manifests and first-failure summaries for each test lane.

Acceptance Criteria:
- CI runs all required jobs on expected triggers.
- Job outputs are sufficient for triage without rerunning everything locally.
- No critical gate is left as manual-only.
- Missing e2e artifacts/manifests fail the workflow.","notes":"2026-02-28: Completed CI workflow hardening for lane manifests/first-failure summaries and deterministic artifact naming. Updated .github/workflows/ci.yml and perf.yml: added per-lane machine-readable manifest emission (build/ci-manifests/*.manifest.json), e2e run_manifest publication enforcement, conformance/profile summary presence checks, fuzz/embedded artifact validation, lint-docs in check lane, deterministic artifact names using github.run_id/run_attempt. Verification via rch offload: make format-check, lint, build, test, conformance, profile-parity all PASS.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:00.106194478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T01:21:14.302625020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T01:21:14.302597949Z","close_reason":"Implemented CI/perf workflow matrix refinements, deterministic artifact naming, lane manifest + first-failure summaries, and manifest/artifact enforcement checks.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","gates","workflows"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.1","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-66l.10","title":"Implement section 10.7 warning/static-analysis/bounded-model gates","description":"Background:
Plan section 10.7 adds mandatory gates replacing lost Rust compiler guarantees in core kernel paths.

Subtasks:
- Enforce warning-as-error policy across GCC/Clang/MSVC with curated high-signal flags.
- Add static-analyzer job and triage policy (must-fix/waiver/backlog) wired into CI outcomes.
- Add bounded model-check job for small-state transition/cancellation/double-resolve properties.
- Publish rerun commands and artifact expectations for each gate in CI docs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Section 10.7 gates run automatically and fail correctly on violations.
- Analyzer waivers are explicit, justified, and auditable.
- Bounded-model artifacts are reproducible and attached to failing runs.","notes":"Section 10.7 gates implemented: (1) tools/ci/run_static_analysis.sh — cppcheck + clang-tidy with ASX_ANALYZER_WAIVER support, JSON reporting; (2) tests/invariant/model_check/test_bounded_model.c — 19 tests over 10 invariants (terminal, forward progress, cancel monotonicity, obligation linearity, spawn prohibition, table completeness, terminal predicates, cancel severity, state names, reachability); (3) Makefile targets: lint-static-analysis, model-check; (4) CI integration in .github/workflows/ci.yml","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:24:59.775986492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T03:21:14.253538281Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T03:21:14.253468431Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","formal","lint","static-analysis"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l.10","depends_on_id":"bd-296.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.10","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.10","depends_on_id":"bd-66l.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.10","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-66l.10","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -268,7 +268,7 @@ The table below maps the crate-root feature/platform restrictions from
 | Upstream contract | Current ANSI C equivalent | State | Evidence / gap |
 |---|---|---|---|
 | `wasm32` must choose exactly one canonical browser profile | `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` exists in `include/asx/asx_config.h`, `src/runtime/profile_compat.c`, the explicit `build-browser` / `PROFILE=BROWSER` lane, and the focused `test-browser-focused` CI/test lane in `Makefile` | `partial` | The browser build/test surface is now explicit and CI-covered, `include/asx/asx_config.h` now hard-fails multiple `ASX_PROFILE_*` selections, and browser builds now expose two compile-time browser subprofiles via `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MODE_COUNT == 2` with default-extended semantics; remaining debt is deeper upstream feature granularity beyond the new minimal/extended split |
-| `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces, compile-time capability macros mark them unavailable, and the browser umbrella stops directly advertising native-only families | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES == 0` under browser builds, and `include/asx/asx.h` now omits direct inclusion of `fs`/`process`/`signal`, the native `app` bootstrap family, `cli`, `server`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `tls`, `db`, `blocking`, and `io_driver` when `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` is active. `include/asx/net/http.h` no longer drags in `fs.h` transitively, browser builds now hide the filesystem-backed `asx_http_serve_static()` helper itself, and `include/asx/fs/fs.h`, `include/asx/process/process.h`, `include/asx/signal/signal.h`, `include/asx/app/app.h`, `include/asx/cli/cli.h`, `include/asx/runtime/io_driver.h`, `include/asx/runtime/blocking.h`, `include/asx/net/server.h`, `include/asx/net/grpc.h`, `include/asx/net/messaging.h`, `include/asx/net/tls.h`, and `include/asx/net/db.h` now self-gate their native families directly; remaining debt is broader mixed-purpose API surfacing plus the lack of finer-grained subprofile removal |
+| `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces, compile-time capability macros mark them unavailable, and the browser umbrella stops directly advertising native-only families | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES == 0` under browser builds, and `include/asx/asx.h` now omits direct inclusion of `fs`/`process`/`signal`, the native `app` bootstrap family, `cli`, `server`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `tls`, `db`, `blocking`, and `io_driver` when `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` is active. `include/asx/net/http.h` no longer drags in `fs.h` transitively, browser builds now hide the filesystem-backed `asx_http_serve_static()` helper itself, `include/asx/app/app.h` no longer drags `fs.h`/`process.h`/`signal.h` outside its native-surface guard, and `include/asx/fs/fs.h`, `include/asx/process/process.h`, `include/asx/signal/signal.h`, `include/asx/app/app.h`, `include/asx/cli/cli.h`, `include/asx/runtime/io_driver.h`, `include/asx/runtime/blocking.h`, `include/asx/net/server.h`, `include/asx/net/grpc.h`, `include/asx/net/messaging.h`, `include/asx/net/tls.h`, and `include/asx/net/db.h` now self-gate their native families directly; remaining debt is broader mixed-purpose API surfacing plus the lack of finer-grained subprofile removal |
 | `browser-io` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Minimal-browser now gates the adapter-based public I/O family at compile time | `partial` | `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO == 0` under `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MINIMAL`, `include/asx/asx.h` omits `bytes/io_adapter`, `encoding`, and `decoding` from the umbrella in that subprofile, the direct `include/asx/bytes/io_adapter.h`, `include/asx/encoding/encoding.h`, and `include/asx/decoding/decoding.h` headers now self-gate there, and focused unit suites plus `test_browser_boundary` prove the split; remaining debt is broader upstream browser feature granularity beyond this adapter-based I/O family |
 | `browser-trace` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Browser minimal-vs-extended subprofiles now gate the public trace family | `partial` | `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_BROWSER_TRACE == 0` under `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MINIMAL`, `include/asx/asx.h` omits `trace`/`telemetry`/`replay`/`lab`/`regression_localize`/`tracing_compat` from the umbrella in minimal browser builds, and the direct headers now self-gate those families unless internal implementation files opt in via `ASX_INTERNAL_TRACE_FAMILY_ACCESS`; remaining debt is broader upstream feature parity beyond this trace-family split |
 | `cli` unsupported on browser builds | Public CLI family exists and is now browser-hidden | `partial` | The C tree ships `include/asx/cli/cli.h` and `src/cli/cli.c`, and browser builds now hide that family both from `include/asx/asx.h` and from direct `include/asx/cli/cli.h` inclusion via `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES`; remaining debt is broader compile-time/browser feature-matrix coverage beyond the CLI surface itself |

@@ -65,13 +65,15 @@ typedef enum {
     ASX_E_WOULD_BLOCK = 701,
     ASX_E_CHANNEL_FULL = 702,
     ASX_E_CHANNEL_NOT_DRAINED = 703,
-    ASX_E_LAGGED = 704, /* broadcast receiver fell behind */
+    ASX_E_LAGGED = 704,        /* broadcast receiver fell behind */
+    ASX_E_CHANNEL_EMPTY = 705, /* channel has no messages (distinct from WOULD_BLOCK) */
 
     /* Timer errors (8xx) */
     ASX_E_TIMER_NOT_FOUND = 800,
     ASX_E_TIMERS_PENDING = 801,
     ASX_E_TIMER_DURATION_EXCEEDED = 802,
-    ASX_E_TIMED_OUT = 803, /* operation exceeded deadline */
+    ASX_E_TIMED_OUT = 803,          /* operation exceeded deadline */
+    ASX_E_THRESHOLD_TIMEOUT = 804, /* quorum/threshold not met within deadline */
 
     /* Quiescence errors (9xx) */
     ASX_E_TASKS_STILL_ACTIVE = 900,
@@ -83,7 +85,9 @@ typedef enum {
 
     /* Resource exhaustion (10xx) */
     ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED = 1000,
-    ASX_E_OVERLOADED = 1001, /* service rejected due to backpressure/load shedding */
+    ASX_E_OVERLOADED = 1001,          /* service rejected due to backpressure/load shedding */
+    ASX_E_COST_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED = 1002, /* cost budget depleted */
+    ASX_E_POLL_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED = 1003, /* poll budget depleted (finer than POLL_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED) */
 
     /* Handle errors (11xx) */
     ASX_E_STALE_HANDLE = 1100,
@@ -103,6 +107,9 @@ typedef enum {
 
     /* Codec equivalence errors (14xx) */
     ASX_E_EQUIVALENCE_MISMATCH = 1400, /* cross-codec semantic mismatch */
+    ASX_E_DUPLICATE_SYMBOL = 1401,     /* RaptorQ: duplicate source symbol */
+    ASX_E_OBJECT_MISMATCH = 1402,      /* RaptorQ: symbol does not belong to object */
+    ASX_E_CORRUPTED_SYMBOL = 1403,     /* RaptorQ: symbol integrity check failed */
 
     /* Trace/replay errors (15xx) */
     ASX_E_REPLAY_MISMATCH = 1500, /* replay continuity check failed */
